title: Two Operational Principles Single Out Quantum Theory
abstract: Quantum theory combines density matrices, Born probabilities, tensor-product composites, positive-operator-valued measures (POVMs), and quantum channels. In a finite-dimensional causal operational theory, we prove that two postulates suffice: local input-output statistics identify channels, and every state admits an equivalent-system purification, unique up to reversible dynamics. The full complex quantum formalism follows; every consistent probability rule is realized as a POVM, so measurement no-restriction is derived rather than assumed.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the entire mathematical structure of finite-dimensional quantum theory can be derived from just two operational principles. By showing that local equivalence and a specific form of purification rule out classical and real-valued theories, it provides a powerful reconstruction of the complex quantum formalism.
main_problem: The paper seeks to determine if the standard complex quantum formalism can be uniquely derived from a minimal set of operational principles rather than being assumed as a single package.
main_result: The author proves that two operational principles—local input-output statistics identify channels and every state admits an equivalent-system purification—are sufficient to uniquely single out finite-dimensional complex quantum theory.
method: The work uses a reconstruction approach within the framework of finite-dimensional Causal Operational Probabilistic Theories (OPTs), employing Euclidean Jordan Algebras and the classification of symmetric cones.
model_or_system: The framework is an abstract, finite-dimensional Causal Operational Probable Theory (OPT) consisting of systems, states, measurements (POVMs), and channels (CPTP maps) defined within a strict monoidal category.
key_observables: Local input-output statistics, measurement outcomes, and probabilities.
important_parameters: Finite-dimensional systems, rank of the algebra, and informational dimension.
main_assumptions: The theory assumes a finite-dimensional causal framework, the existence of deterministic effects, and the validity of the two proposed operational principles.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the reconstruction process from a general OPT to quantum theory; Table I compares classical, real quantum, and complex quantum theories against the two postulates.
paper_structure: The paper follows a logical derivation: establishing the framework of OPTs, applying the two postulates to derive the geometry of state spaces (symmetric cones), proving the no-restriction hypothesis for measurements, and finally using the classification of Jordan algebras to identify the theory as complex quantum theory.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for researchers in quantum foundations and open quantum systems as it provides a fundamental justification for the use of density matrices, POVMs, and CPTP maps, showing they are necessary consequences of basic operational symmetries.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic picture of the reconstruction. In a standard OPT, states, measurements, composites, and channels can in principle be specified separately. Once local equivalence and ES purification are imposed, these ingredients are fixed to the standard complex quantum structure: density matrices, POVMs, the Born rule, tensor- product composition, and CPTP maps.
